content.text:
---
name: brave-search
description: Search the web using Brave Search API. Use this skill when you need to find information, documentation, or answers that are not in the local codebase.
---

# Brave Search Skill

This skill provides web search capabilities using the Brave Search API.

## Prerequisites

- [Brave Search API Key](https://api.search.brave.com/app/dashboard)
- `BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY` environment variable set in `~/.pi-secrets/.env`
- `jq` installed for JSON processing

## Usage

The agent can use the following commands to search the web or read specific pages.

### Search Web
```bash
./search.sh "your search query"
```

### Search and Output as Markdown Table
```bash
./search.sh "your search query" --table
```

### Read a Specific URL
```bash
./read.sh "https://example.com"
```

### Search and Get Summaries
```bash
./search.sh "your search query" --summarize
```

## Implementation

The skill uses a helper script `search.sh` to interact with the API.

---
name: brave-search
description: Search the web using Brave Search API. Use this skill when you need to find information, documentation, or answers that are not in the local codebase.
---

# Brave Search Skill

This skill provides web search capabilities using the Brave Search API.

## Prerequisites

- [Brave Search API Key](https://api.search.brave.com/app/dashboard)
- `BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY` environment variable set in `~/.pi-secrets/.env`
- `jq` installed for JSON processing

## Usage

The agent can use the following commands to search the web or read specific pages.

### Search Web
```bash
./search.sh "your search query"
```

### Search and Output as Markdown Table
```bash
./search.sh "your search query" --table
```

### Read a Specific URL
```bash
./read.sh "https://example.com"
```

### Search and Get Summaries
```bash
./search.sh "your search query" --summarize
```

## Implementation

The skill uses a helper script `search.sh` to interact with the API.

content.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash

URL="$1"
if [ -z "$URL" ]; then
  echo "Usage: ./read.sh <url>"
  exit 1
fi

# We use r.jina.ai to get a clean markdown version of the page.
# This is a common and effective way for AI agents to "read" web pages.
curl -s "https://r.jina.ai/$URL"

details.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash

URL="$1"
if [ -z "$URL" ]; then
  echo "Usage: ./read.sh <url>"
  exit 1
fi

# We use r.jina.ai to get a clean markdown version of the page.
# This is a common and effective way for AI agents to "read" web pages.
curl -s "https://r.jina.ai/$URL"

content.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash

# Check for API key
if [ -z "$BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY" ]; then
  echo "Error: BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY environment variable is not set."
  echo "Please add it to your ~/.pi-secrets/.env file."
  exit 1
fi

QUERY="$1"
if [ -z "$QUERY" ]; then
  echo "Usage: ./search.sh \"query\" [--summarize] [--table]"
  exit 1
fi

# Flags
shift || true
SUMMARIZE=false
TABLE=false
for arg in "$@"; do
  case "$arg" in
    --summarize) SUMMARIZE=true ;;
    --table) TABLE=true ;;
  esac
done

# Brave Search API endpoint
ENDPOINT="https://api.search.brave.com/res/v1/web/search"

# Perform search
RESPONSE=$(curl -s -G "$ENDPOINT" \
  --data-urlencode "q=$QUERY" \
  -H "Accept: application/json" \
  -H "X-Subscription-Token: $BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY")

# Check for errors in response
if echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -e '.message' > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "API Error: $(echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -r '.message')"
  exit 1
fi

# If table output requested, render Markdown table of web results
if [ "$TABLE" = true ]; then
  echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -r '
    if .web and .web.results then
      .web.results | to_entries | ( ["| Rank | Title | URL | Description |","|---:|---|---|---:|"] ), ( .[] | "| " + ((.key+1)|tostring) + " | " + (.value.title // "") | gsub("\n"; " ") | gsub("\|"; "\\|") + " | " + (.value.url // "") + " | " + ((.value.description // "") | gsub("\n"; " ") | gsub("\|"; "\\|")) + " |" )
    else
      "No web.results in response"
    end
  '
  exit 0
fi

# Default human-readable output
echo "--- RESULTS FOR: $QUERY ---"

# Check for Infobox
INFOBOX=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -r '.infobox // empty')
if [ -n "$INFOBOX" ]; then
  echo "### INFOBOX ###"
  echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -r '.infobox.content.title + ": " + .infobox.content.description'
  echo "---"
fi

# Check for FAQ
FAQ=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -r '.faq.results // empty')
if [ -n "$FAQ" ]; then
  echo "### FAQ ###"
  echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -r '.faq.results[] | "Q: " + .question + "\nA: " + .answer + "\n---"'
fi

# Web Results
echo "### WEB RESULTS ###"
echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -r '
  .web.results[] | 
  "Title: " + .title + "\nURL: " + .url + "\nDescription: " + .description + "\n---"
'

#!/usr/bin/env bash

# Check for API key
if [ -z "$BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY" ]; then
  echo "Error: BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY environment variable is not set."
  echo "Please add it to your ~/.pi-secrets/.env file."
  exit 1
fi

QUERY="$1"
if [ -z "$QUERY" ]; then
  echo "Usage: ./search.sh \"query\" [--summarize] [--table]"
  exit 1
fi

# Flags
shift || true
SUMMARIZE=false
TABLE=false
for arg in "$@"; do
  case "$arg" in
    --summarize) SUMMARIZE=true ;;
    --table) TABLE=true ;;
  esac
done

# Brave Search API endpoint
ENDPOINT="https://api.search.brave.com/res/v1/web/search"

# Perform search
RESPONSE=$(curl -s -G "$ENDPOINT" \
  --data-urlencode "q=$QUERY" \
  -H "Accept: application/json" \
  -H "X-Subscription-Token: $BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY")

# Check for errors in response
if echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -e '.message' > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "API Error: $(echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -r '.message')"
  exit 1
fi

# If table output requested, render Markdown table of web results
if [ "$TABLE" = true ]; then
  echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -r '
    if .web and .web.results then
      .web.results | to_entries | ( ["| Rank | Title | URL | Description |","|---:|---|---|---:|"] ), ( .[] | "| " + ((.key+1)|tostring) + " | " + (.value.title // "") | gsub("\n"; " ") | gsub("\|"; "\\|") + " | " + (.value.url // "") + " | " + ((.value.description // "") | gsub("\n"; " ") | gsub("\|"; "\\|")) + " |" )
    else
      "No web.results in response"
    end
  '
  exit 0
fi

# Default human-readable output
echo "--- RESULTS FOR: $QUERY ---"

# Check for Infobox
INFOBOX=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -r '.infobox // empty')
if [ -n "$INFOBOX" ]; then
  echo "### INFOBOX ###"
  echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -r '.infobox.content.title + ": " + .infobox.content.description'
  echo "---"
fi

# Check for FAQ
FAQ=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -r '.faq.results // empty')
if [ -n "$FAQ" ]; then
  echo "### FAQ ###"
  echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -r '.faq.results[] | "Q: " + .question + "\nA: " + .answer + "\n---"'
fi

# Web Results
echo "### WEB RESULTS ###"
echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -r '
  .web.results[] | 
  "Title: " + .title + "\nURL: " + .url + "\nDescription: " + .description + "\n---"
'

## From the magazine:[The Pelicans Can No Longer Reach You](https://www.poetryfoundation.org/poetrymagazine/poems/1779656/the-pelicans-can-no-longer-reach-you)

By Golden

The pelicans can no longer reach you

Watching droughts from windows

Arctic snow shining shingles

Fire cutting…

Poem

## From the magazine:[Grief Lessons](https://www.poetryfoundation.org/poetrymagazine/poems/1779588/grief-lessons)

By Emily Skaja

No one stole your money.

No one stole your whole heart

then climbed into a stolen plane

and stole away …

Poem

## From the magazine:[Y.](https://www.poetryfoundation.org/poetrymagazine/poems/1779596/y)

By Luis Muñoz

Translated By Idra Novey&Garth Greenwell

Not one thing or the other.

Not equal nor different.

Given that he had spent

the night

removing the dots…

## Poem of the day

### From the magazine:[I.](https://www.poetryfoundation.org/poetrymagazine/poems/1779608/i)

Translated By Idra Novey&Garth Greenwell

He lies if they ask him

how he does it.

The Poetry Foundation transforms lives through the power of words. Our work aims to amplify poetry and celebrate poets by fostering spaces for all to create, experience, and share poetry.

### Linda Gregg

1942—2019

### Linda Gregg

 B. 1942

Linda Gregg was born in New York and raised in Marin County, California. She earned both a BA and an MA from San Francisco State University. Gregg published several collections of poetry, including _All of It Singing: New and Selected Poems_(2008), a _Los Angeles Times_ Favorite Book of 2008 and winner of the Poetry Society of America’s William Carlos Williams Award; _In the Middle Distance_(2006);_Things and Flesh_ (1999), finalist for the Kingsley Tufts...

By Noah Baldino&The Editors

April 7, 2023

Work by the Rocker, Poet, Activist, and Literary Trendsetter

Poem

## From the magazine:[The Pelicans Can No Longer Reach You](https://www.poetryfoundation.org/poetrymagazine/poems/1779656/the-pelicans-can-no-longer-reach-you)

By Golden

The pelicans can no longer reach you

Watching droughts from windows

Arctic snow shining shingles

Fire cutting…

Poem

## From the magazine:[Grief Lessons](https://www.poetryfoundation.org/poetrymagazine/poems/1779588/grief-lessons)

By Emily Skaja

No one stole your money.

No one stole your whole heart

then climbed into a stolen plane

and stole away …

Poem

## From the magazine:[Y.](https://www.poetryfoundation.org/poetrymagazine/poems/1779596/y)

By Luis Muñoz

Translated By Idra Novey&Garth Greenwell

Not one thing or the other.

Not equal nor different.

Given that he had spent

the night

removing the dots…

## Poem of the day

### From the magazine:[I.](https://www.poetryfoundation.org/poetrymagazine/poems/1779608/i)

By Luis Muñoz

Translated By Idra Novey&Garth Greenwell

[Read More](https://www.poetryfoundation.org/poetrymagazine/poems/1779608/i)Listen

He lies if they ask him

how he does it.

...

The Poetry Foundation transforms lives through the power of words. Our work aims to amplify poetry and celebrate poets by fostering spaces for all to create, experience, and share poetry.

[How We Fulfill Our Mission](https://www.poetryfoundation.org/about-us)

* * *

## Featured Poets

Linda Gregg Sara Elkamel Erika L. Sánchez CAConrad

![Image 13: Photo of poet Linda Gregg](https://cdn-test.poetryfoundation.org/cdn-cgi/image/w=10,h=10,q=50,fit=cover,blur=3/content/images/13846d7d7f187ecd7eef643955d0eee73ba2150b.jpeg)

### Linda Gregg

1942—2019

### Linda Gregg

 B. 1942

Linda Gregg was born in New York and raised in Marin County, California. She earned both a BA and an MA from San Francisco State University. Gregg published several collections of poetry, including _All of It Singing: New and Selected Poems_(2008), a _Los Angeles Times_ Favorite Book of 2008 and winner of the Poetry Society of America’s William Carlos Williams Award; _In the Middle Distance_(2006);_Things and Flesh_ (1999), finalist for the Kingsley Tufts...

[Read More](https://www.poetryfoundation.org/poets/linda-gregg)

## Connect with us!

Daily poems, poetry events, essays, and more from the Poetry Foundation delivered to your inbox.

 Sign Up 

First Name* 

Last Name* 

Email* 

- [x] 
Sign me up for:*   - [x] _Poem of the Day_ 
Start each day with a poem delivered to your inbox! Poems are selected by Poetry Foundation editors and guests to correspond with historic events, poet anniversaries, and more from _Poetry_ magazine and the poem archive at PoetryFoundation.org.  
*   - [x] Poetry Foundation Weekly Newsletter 
Access exclusive content from the latest issue of _Poetry_ magazine, read poetry-related essays, stay up to date on Foundation news, and more, every Wednesday.  
*   - [x] Events 
Keep up with the Poetry Foundation’s poetry readings, workshops, exhibitions, and other events, sent every other Friday.  
*   - [x] Educators 
Monthly during the school year, receive periodic poetry resources and events curated specifically for young people, lifelong learners, and those who work with them.  
*   - [x] Press Releases 
Receive newsworthy updates from the Poetry Foundation.  

 Subscribe 

By clicking "Subscribe," you agree to The Poetry Foundation's collection and use of your personal information submitted through this form in accordance with our [Terms of Service](https://www.poetryfoundation.org/terms-of-use/) and [Privacy Policy](https://www.poetryfoundation.org/privacy-policy/), which may include use with our online advertising programs.

content.text:
Done — I used the brave-search skill's read.sh to fetch a cleaned markdown version of https://www.poetryfoundation.org/.

Quick results
- Title: Poetry Foundation
- URL: https://www.poetryfoundation.org/
- Fetched (markdown) snapshot via r.jina.ai

Highlights / summary
- Main site navigation: Poems & Poets; Topics & Themes; Features; Grants & Programs; About Us; Poetry magazine.
- Featured content on the homepage: Editors’ Picks, Latest, Opportunities, and themed collections (e.g., Arab American Heritage Month).
- Recent pieces called out: interviews and articles (e.g., an interview with Jack White), poem samplers, and multiple poems from Poetry magazine (Poem of the Day, selections, etc.).
- Calls to action: subscribe to Poem of the Day and Poetry magazine, submit to Poetry magazine, and sign up for newsletters.
- Resources: collections, poem guides, poem archive, poets directory, podcasts, books, videos, events, grants, and library info.
- Footer / contact info: Poetry Foundation address in Chicago, links to contact, privacy, terms, mobile app, and social links.

If you want, I can:
- Paste the full fetched markdown here,
- Extract specific sections (Poem of the Day, Featured Poets, About Us, submissions guidelines, etc.),
- Search the Poetry Foundation site for a particular poet, poem, or topic.

Which would you like next?
